summary: Sprint T5 — UI Calibration Layer + Epistemic Transparency. Frontend-only sprint (new IntelligenceScreen + CalibrationSection in DeploymentScreen/TradeScreen + intel toggle in TradingHeader). Per request, backend regression was the primary validation. RESULT: 32/32 PASS on /app/backend/tests/test_sprint_t4_calibration.py (PYTHONPATH=/app/backend pytest) — including the previously-failing TestVerdictCalibrationBlock::test_verdict_has_calibration which now correctly returns appliedAdjustment='none_wait_verdict' for WAIT verdicts. All T1/T2/T3/T4 endpoints 200, terminal honest-404, miniapp/admin 200, scheduler running. All 8 frontend artifact checks PASS — strict vocabulary respected (zero forbidden words), correct DOM/JSX layering (CalibrationSection between MODULE ALIGNMENT and EXECUTION STRUCTURE), IntelligenceScreen has the 3 mandated SectionHeader titles, TradingShell routes 'INTELLIGENCE' tab, TradingHeader has school-icon toggle with correct testID, app-mode.store TradingTab includes 'INTELLIGENCE', and trading-runtime-api.ts TradingVerdict.calibration interface contains every required subfield. Expo bundle HTTP 200.

frontend_artifact_checks.IntelligenceScreen.tsx vocabulary (good/: PASS — only match is the FORBIDDEN list inside the file's own header docstring (lines 7-10), which is a rule definition, not a descriptive usage. All status strings use allowed phrases: 'sample still limited', 'reliability emerging', 'historically weak follow-through', 'historical instability', 'mixed historical follow-through', 'historical follow-through has held up'. RELIABILITY_LABEL map uses: 'weak sample', 'emerging reliability', 'usable reliability', 'strong historical reliability'.
frontend_artifact_checks.TradeScreen.tsx CalibrationSection vocab: PASS — zero forbidden words anywhere in file.
frontend_artifact_checks.IntelligenceScreen.tsx 3 SectionHeader t: PASS — lines 182, 196, 210: 'A · HISTORICAL RELIABILITY', 'B · TOXIC STRUCTURES', 'C · STABLE STRUCTURES'.
frontend_artifact_checks.TradeScreen.tsx CalibrationSection JSX p: PASS — TradeScreen.tsx:163 MODULE ALIGNMENT block, :179 CalibrationSection rendered, :185 EXECUTION STRUCTURE block, :201 BLOCKED block. Order is correct.
frontend_artifact_checks.TradingShell.tsx imports + routes INTELL: PASS — line 12 imports IntelligenceScreen; lines 47-48 route case 'INTELLIGENCE' → <IntelligenceScreen />.
frontend_artifact_checks.TradingHeader.tsx intel-btn with school: PASS — line 59 onPress toggles tradingTab COMMAND↔INTELLIGENCE; line 62 testID='trading-header-intel-btn'; line 65 icon='school' (active) / 'school-outline' (inactive).
frontend_artifact_checks.trading-runtime-api.ts TradingVerdict.ca: PASS — lines 42-60: contains sample, wins, losses, winRate, targetRate, stopRate, avgPnlPct, avgBarsHeld, reliability, alignmentBucket, risk, appliedAdjustment, note. All required fields present.

backend_regression.pytest_t4_full_suite: 32/32 PASSED (was 31/32 in iter6 — WAIT bug fixed)
backend_regression.GET /api/trading/verdict/BTC: 200, calibration block present with sample=0, reliability=weak_sample, bucket=0.33_0.67, appliedAdjustment=none_wait_verdict (correct for WAIT)
backend_regression.GET /api/trading/intelligence/calibratio: 200, totalSample=6, reliability=emerging, warnings=['insufficient_data (sample=6/10)'], thresholds={observe_only_max:5, warn_only_max:10, soft_adjust_max:25, hard_gate_min:25}, buckets_count=1

action_items: None. T5 acceptance is fully green. Backend has zero regression vs T4; the iter6 WAIT-verdict bug has been fixed (verdict.calibration.appliedAdjustment now correctly returns 'none_wait_verdict' for WAIT verdicts). All 8 frontend artifact checks pass with strict vocabulary compliance.

(Optional, non-blocking) When running the T4 pytest suite, use `PYTHONPATH=/app/backend pytest backend/tests/test_sprint_t4_calibration.py` — the test file does `from services import calibration as _calib` which only resolves with the backend on PYTHONPATH. Consider adding a conftest.py at /app/backend/tests/ that prepends /app/backend to sys.path, so the suite is portable from any CWD.
critical_code_review_comments: IntelligenceScreen.tsx is well-designed: pure function of /api/trading/intelligence/calibration response, refresh-controlled, error-state handled, empty-state handled per section, dashed empty boxes, sample-gated tone (warn/ok), and importantly the BucketCard status line uses ONLY allowed vocabulary based on sample/winRate thresholds (lines 264-277).
TradingHeader school-icon toggle is wired correctly via tradingTab state. testID is present.
TradeScreen.tsx CalibrationSection ordering in JSX (lines 163→179→185→201) matches the spec: MODULE ALIGNMENT → CALIBRATION LAYER → EXECUTION STRUCTURE → BLOCKED.
trading-runtime-api.ts calibration interface covers all 8 required appliedAdjustment string-union variants including 'none_wait_verdict' and 'pending' — defensive against backend evolution.
RELIABILITY_LABEL map (IntelligenceScreen.tsx:63-68) is the single source of truth for reliability strings shown to user — clean.

Minor concern: IntelligenceScreen.tsx makeStyles() colors.textMuted is referenced but if useColors() doesn't provide it, will fall through to undefined string — falls back gracefully because of the `|| '#888'` defaults inside reliabilityColor(). Not blocking.
Sprint T4 backend hard-gate path (apply_to_verdict) — the WAIT short-circuit fix is now in place; suite is fully green.

success_rate.frontend: 100% artifact checks (8/8); UI navigation flow not tested per request (gated behind app-mode + capability switch)

seed_data_creation: Pytest suite seeds synthetic TEST_/ZZTEST/ZZSOFT/ZZHARD/ZZOBS/ZZWARN outcome rows with _seedTag='T4_TEST' and cleans up via teardown fixtures. One real BTC outcome remains in trading_outcomes_v2 from the writeback test (intentional). No new seeding done by this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint T5 is fully green. Backend has zero regression — all 32 T4 pytest tests pass, the iter6 WAIT-verdict appliedAdjustment bug is FIXED. All 8 frontend artifact checks pass with strict vocabulary compliance. For any future testing of the IntelligenceScreen UI itself (Playwright), note that it sits behind the TradingShell tradingTab='INTELLIGENCE' state which is reached via TradingHeader testID='trading-header-intel-btn', AFTER the user has already entered TRADING mode from INTELLIGENCE mode via the app-mode switch. Easiest Playwright entry is to mutate the Zustand store directly via `page.evaluate(() => window.__APP_MODE__.setMode('TRADING'))` then toggle the intel btn — but that's overkill for T5 since IntelligenceScreen is purely a function of /api/trading/intelligence/calibration which T4 already validates.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no issues found in this iteration. The iter6 WAIT-verdict bug (apply_to_verdict overwriting 'none_wait_verdict' with 'observe_only') has been fixed by main agent between iter6 and iter7; verdict/BTC now correctly returns appliedAdjustment='none_wait_verdict' when action='WAIT'.
